name: Lab Interpretation — Basic Glucose + HbA1c
description: Level 1 — handcrafted — single metabolic abnormality, uncontrolled DM type 2
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 62-year-old female with known type 2 diabetes at her routine clinic visit:

- Fasting Blood Glucose: 285 mg/dL (reference: 70–100 mg/dL)
- HbA1c: 9.2% (reference: <5.7% normal, 5.7–6.4% prediabetes, ≥6.5% diabetes; target for controlled DM: <7.0%)
- Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL (reference: 0.5–1.1)
- eGFR: 72 mL/min/1.73m² (reference: >60 normal)
- Urine microalbumin: 45 mg/g creatinine (reference: <30 normal)

Provide clinical interpretation of these results.

abnormal_values.significance: Severely elevated glucose indicating markedly uncontrolled hyperglycemia
3-month average glucose severely above target, indicating poorly controlled diabetes for at least 3 months
Microalbuminuria indicating early diabetic nephropathy / renal involvement

expected.interpretation: Poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus with HbA1c 9.2% and fasting glucose 285 mg/dL. Early diabetic nephropathy evidenced by microalbuminuria. Renal function currently preserved (eGFR 72, creatinine normal).
expected.pattern_recognition: Uncontrolled DM pattern: high fasting glucose (285) + high HbA1c (9.2%) + microalbuminuria as early end-organ damage. eGFR still in normal range but microalbuminuria signals impending nephropathy.
